evidence.quote: The failed flight unit had been reworked, but the manufacturing procedural change had not been incorporated into the rework process.
An identical problem had been previously discovered that prompted a change to the manufacturing procedures
AC2 buss phase a voltage fluctuated between 112 and 122V for one minute

impact_notes: Required inverter replacement during prelaunch checkout, causing schedule impact for STS-36 mission

evidence.quote: A pin solder joint was found to be fractured. The unit was repaired without investigating the cause of the joint failure.
Repairing a failed component before investigating the cause of a failure can destroy evidence important to the investigation.
Rework procedures were revised to require an investigation of the cause of a failure before any attempt to repair the failed component.

impact_notes: Loss of critical failure investigation evidence, reduced probability of implementing effective recurrence controls

evidence.quote: Use of batteries that have not completed qualification testing caused employees to receive caustic electrolyte exposure
Batteries are of a new design still under qualification testing
Institute requirements that will prevent the use of batteries that have not completed qualification testing

impact_notes: Personnel contamination requiring medical evaluation and immediate cleanup procedures

evidence.quote: The orbiter Reaction Control System (RCS) thruster valves continue to leak in flight
Procedural changes have improved thruster reliability, and the incidence of leakage has been reduced but not eliminated
accelerated schedule for the OSP program necessitated that requirements development be conducted before the OSP teams were fully staffed
This resulted in incomplete requirements that lead to later updates and changes

impact_notes: Incomplete requirements led to later updates and changes, impacting schedule and rework costs

evidence.quote: A failed hydraulic analog pressure gauge, caused by inadequate solder adhesion between the gauge bourdon tube and socket.
Employees could not see inside pump house during operation and therfore did not easily discover the leak to shut the system down.
KSC did not have requirements to assess worst-case consequences from fluid leaks.

impact_notes: $500,000 in electrical equipment replacement costs, no personnel injuries, no mission/launch schedule impact

evidence.quote: the Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) did not identify the hazard
LNG has a density much greater than gaseous hydrogen, so produces much higher momentum loads
complacency related to the vent line developed over time. The vent line was not maintained or inspected

impact_notes: Significant structural damage to vent piping, expandable joint assembly, and support structures requiring facility repairs

evidence.quote: center coordinators at GRC do not have any assigned time to perform NANADARTS duties, and there exists no WBS, which leads to assigned work taking precedence
accumulation of a massive backlog of NANADARTS documents which was comprised of documents that went undistributed by center coordinators
Distribution of documents from the agency coordinator to the center coordinators is irregular...making it difficult to develop a regular routine

impact_notes: Massive backlog of safety-critical GIDEP notices and NASA advisories over 3 years, compromising closed-loop reporting requirements per NPR 8735.1

evidence.quote: Materials exposed to insufficient concentrations of the suppressant burned faster than the same materials burning with no suppressant at all
Testing of the suppressant with the materials of concern revealed the described issues
The vehicle design used a commercial-off-the-shelf PFE containing HFC-227ea

impact_notes: Fire suppressant system actually increases fire hazard in spacecraft environment, creating significant safety risk for crew

evidence.quote: pipe elbow was improperly glued together when it was originally installed 40 years ago
chilled water campus loop had been leaking for years and multiple attempts to find the source of the leak were not successful
PVC should not be used for chilled water piping either outside, inside or under facilities

impact_notes: Over $1.5M in facility damages, chilled water plant shutdown, loss of cooling to essential buildings including Launch Control Center

evidence.quote: disruption of multiple launch vehicle Ethernet communication links occurred during ascent at an altitude of between 10 km and 15 km
exacerbated by the lack of adequate cable shielding that affected flight computer communication links
assume triboelectric charging on vehicle surfaces will occur during ascent and possibly descent. Develop a mitigation strategy

impact_notes: Multiple redundant Ethernet communication links between spacecraft and launch vehicle were disrupted during critical ascent phase

evidence.quote: engaging the engineering team's focus on the requirements sell-off was challenging
continuous communication between the verification stakeholders needs to occur
FireX MFP supply was swapped from gaseous nitrogen to high pressure air...The TEA-TEB system was inactive at the time of the configuration change
Station 6 description did not state that Station 6 was serving as a purge to the TEA-TEB System
MFP drawing and panel labeling was not changed to reflect the additional purpose of Station 6 for TEA-TEB purging

impact_notes: No personal injury or facility damage sustained, but pyrophoric material contamination created safety hazard

evidence.quote: the migration license was not updated to the production license
improve our communication for license upgrades and plan the steps involved

impact_notes: 6 hours downtime for workflow system acquisition of new licenses

evidence.quote: It does not specifically address space systems (e.g., rovers) that are operational on other surfaces, and it does not address associated hardware categories
rework instructions lack requirements for set screw torquing and verification by a quality inspector
precision regulator had been reworked twice during its build to repair excessive vent port leakage
stem screw rotating and decaying the output of the precision regulator

impact_notes: Complete mission loss - satellite declared a loss by the Navy due to unacceptable orbit insertion

evidence.quote: Failure to secure/tether a draeger pump during toxic vapor checks resulted in a "close call"
draeger pump was pulled from the platform and fell, striking the orbiter's FRCS blanket area

impact_notes: Equipment fell and struck orbiter FRCS blanket area, potential damage to critical flight hardware

evidence.quote: Potential International Space Station (ISS) Supportability Problems With Existing Extravehicular Activity (EVA) Assets
initiatives cover acquisition of material, development of procedures, and improved training
Expedite completion of the planned initiatives related to the safety of EVA so that maximum benefit can be realized during the upcoming intensive ISS assembly schedule
Schedule and/or budget pressures could lead to deferring work to orbit or curtailing prelaunch testing.
there is little, if any, schedule slack to accommodate late or unavailable hardware

impact_notes: Potential decrease in prelaunch ground testing due to schedule/budget pressures, with work deferred to orbit

evidence.quote: Three SCP failures were attributed to intermittent or continuous short circuits caused by the growth of conductive filaments, known as tin whiskers
continuous process monitoring (especially of suppliers) must occur to assure that tin whisker growth is addressed
Although the tin whisker phenomenon has been documented for decades and is reasonably well understood, it remains a reliability hazard

impact_notes: One satellite removed from service, multiple spacecraft control processor failures across three commercial communications satellites

evidence.quote: The incident was attributed to the connector mating configuration-the exposed position of a corner pin during a slightly twisted entry.
avoid mating or demating any powered connector, whether powered externally or by a spacecraft battery
Use scoop-proof power connectors

impact_notes: No damage to flight hardware due to adequate surge rating of blocking diodes and inductors

evidence.quote: The pre-flight analysis to recognize or predict the effects and uncertainties from both the unbalanced thrusters and the impingement was inadequate.
If impingement is suspected, a test similar to the Voyager impingement test should be planned and executed early in the flight.
unexpected translational velocity increments and large non-gravitational acceleration effects were observed

impact_notes: Required additional orbit determination analysis, testing, modeling, and new operational interfaces between teams throughout flight

evidence.quote: Unexpected icing of the single-string unheated Pitot-static air data source resulted in the aircraft departing controlled flight and crashing
Consider the use of a redundant air data source (one of diverse design so as to lessen the probability of common cause failure)
inherently unstable and dependent on air data for dynamic stability

impact_notes: Catastrophic loss of aircraft due to departure from controlled flight

evidence.quote: Because of late discovery of this issue, the new procedure was not verified against the math model.
The procedural change was coordinated and signed by all the proper personnel but was not run against the math model because it was thought to be well understood.
All procedural changes to cryogenic loading procedures must be verified against the math model.

impact_notes: Launch hold at T-31 seconds, potential catastrophic LOX feedline geyser risk

evidence.quote: Similar adjacent connections on the ECS pressure distribution system allow for the possibility of misconnected lines
Pressure lines could be misconnected and result in damage to flight hardware or injury to personnel

impact_notes: Potential damage to flight hardware or injury to personnel from misconnected pressure lines

evidence.quote: Visual observation must be maintained at all times during hoisting operations due to extremely limited clearances
Maintain crane lift cables vertical and sling assembly level to prevent excessive loads imposed on stabilizing frame
Poor packaging design and an inadequate insulation scheme allows numerous places for electrical shorts
In-flight temperature extremes...were greater than experienced by previous NOAA satellites...and were near qualification temperature limits
thermal expansion and contraction in excess of previous flights...caused mounting bolts protruding through heat sink to penetrate isolation

impact_notes: Complete loss of satellite - all contact lost within six orbits, no downlink signal thereafter detected

evidence.quote: louvers were removed after Thermal Vacuum testing and reinstalled as 'Non-flight / Temporary mechanical installation' for fit check purposes. The connector was not mated
This connector was not on the list. The NOAA-K satellite was flown with the thermal louver cable disconnected.
Keep good records of all 'non-flight' installations. Discrepancy reports, red tags and/or a configuration log book should be mandated

impact_notes: AVHRR instrument running hotter than normal, thermal control system non-functional
